details: The "Financial Assistance for Marriage" scheme, introduced by the Himachal Pradesh Building and Other Construction Workers Welfare Board (HPBOCWWB), provides ₹51,000/- for the marriage of a registered unmarried beneficiary and ₹51,000/- per child for the marriage of up to two children of a married beneficiary, offering essential financial support to construction workers and their families.
benefits: ₹51,000/- for a registered unmarried beneficiary’s marriage.
₹51,000/- per child for up to two children of a registered married beneficiary.
eligibility: For Registration as a Building/Construction Worker:
The applicant should be a resident of Himachal Pradesh.
The applicant should be a Building or Construction Worker.
The age of the applicant should be between 18 and 60 years.
The applicant should have worked in building or construction activities for at least 90 days in the preceding 12 months.
The registration should be renewed annually.
The worker’s membership with the Welfare Board must be active at the time of application.
The worker should be unmarried at the time of applying for the financial assistance.
The worker should have at least a two month subscription.
The benefit is provided for the marriage of up to two children of a registered married worker.

online: The Registration Process for Building and Other Construction Workers is as follows:
The interested applicant should visit the Labor Welfare Office (during office hours) and request a hard copy of the prescribed application form from the concerned authority.
fill in all the mandatory fields, paste a passport-sized photograph (signed across, if required), and attach copies of all the mandatory documents (self-attested, if required).

Submit the duly filled and signed application form along with a wage slip or appointment letter from the employer, verifying 90 days of construction work. If unavailable, a certificate from a local authority (such as a councilor, executive officer, or Panchayat Secretary) can be submitted to verify employment.
After submitting the documents and payment, the worker will receive an acknowledgment receipt confirming their registration.
Ensure that the application is submitted within the prescribed period, if any.
After three years, the worker must visit the office to pay a renewal fee and submit proof of 90 days of work each year to maintain eligibility for benefits.
A one-time registration fee must be paid at the office, and it will be valid for three years.

documents_required: Identity proof i.e. Aadhaar Card, Voter Card
Bank account details/copy of bank passbook

faqs.question: What is the Financial Assistance for Marriage Scheme?
faqs.answer: This scheme provides financial assistance for the marriage of a registered unmarried beneficiary and for the marriage of up to two children of a registered married beneficiary under the HPBOCWWB.

faqs.question: How much financial assistance is provided under this scheme?
faqs.answer: A financial assistance of ₹51,000 is provided for the marriage of a registered unmarried beneficiary and ₹51,000 per child for up to two children of a registered married beneficiary.

faqs.question: Who is eligible for the marriage assistance for unmarried workers?
faqs.answer: The worker must be unmarried, engaged in building or construction work, and registered with the Himachal Pradesh Building and Other Construction Workers Welfare Board (HPBOCWWB).

faqs.question: Can I apply for the marriage assistance if I am already married?
faqs.answer: No, this financial assistance is only available for unmarried workers at the time of application.

faqs.question: What is the eligibility for receiving marriage assistance for children?
faqs.answer: The parent must be a registered worker in the building or construction sector under HPBOCWWB, and the parent’s membership must be active at the time of application. The scheme covers up to two children.

faqs.question: How long should a worker be registered with HPBOCWWB before applying?
faqs.answer: The worker should have at least a two-month subscription to the HPBOCWWB before applying for the assistance.

faqs.question: Can a worker receive marriage assistance for more than two children?
faqs.answer: No, the scheme provides assistance for the marriage of up to two children of a registered married worker.

faqs.question: What is the process for registering with HPBOCWWB?
faqs.answer: The applicant must be between 18 and 60 years of age, possess a certificate confirming completion of at least 90 days of building and construction work, and register either at the Labour Welfare Office or through self-registration on the official portal.

faqs.question: How can I check if my membership with HPBOCWWB is active?
faqs.answer: You can check your active membership status by visiting the HPBOCWWB office or logging into the official portal.

faqs.question: Is there any age limit to apply for the marriage assistance?
faqs.answer: There is no specific age limit for married beneficiaries applying for child marriage assistance, but the worker applying for their own marriage assistance must be unmarried.
